perturbation.original_text: The disclosing Party shall identify the written Other Confidential Information or any other Confidential Information disclosed in tangible media by marketing it "Confidential" or "Proprietary" or "Secret" or words of similar import. Other Confidential Information disclosed orally shall be thereafter summarized by the Disclosing party and the summary provided to the Receiving Party clearly identifying that such information is other party that was not known by such party or commonly known in the public domain shall be presumed to be other confidential for purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The disclosing Party shall identify the written Other Confidential Information or any other Confidential Information disclosed in tangible media. Other Confidential Information disclosed orally shall be thereafter summarized by the Disclosing party and the summary provided to the Receiving Party clearly identifying that such information is other party that was not known by such party or commonly known in the public domain shall be presumed to be other confidential for purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the marking of "Confidential" or "Proprietary" or "Secret", the definition of how written confidential information is to be identified is now vague. This creates an in-text contradiction because, without proper marking, it becomes unclear which documents are actually considered 'Other Confidential Information,' making the obligation to protect them ambiguous, especially when Section 3 requires reasonable care to prevent unauthorized disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Restriction on Access. The Receiving Party shall to use all reasonable care, but in no event less than the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information of similar importance, to prevent the unauthorized use, disclosure, publication or dissemination of the other Party's Other Confidential Information (and any concepts, inventions or other information derived there from). Each party shall restrict access to the other party's other confidential information (and any concepts, invention or other information derived there from) to only those of its employees, representatives or advisors to whom such access is reasonably necessary or appropriate for carrying out the permitted use of such other confidential information. Each party shall to obtain Non-Disclosure Agreements containing appropriate restrictions on the persons signing the same prior to disclosing other confidential information (or any concepts, inventions or other information derived there from) to such persons.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a clear mechanism for identifying 'Other Confidential Information' through specific markings ('Confidential,' 'Proprietary,' 'Secret'). The changed text removes this explicit marking requirement, making the identification of such information vague. This directly contradicts the 'Restriction on Access' clause, which requires the Receiving Party to protect the 'Other Confidential Information'. Without a clear marking system as outlined in the original text, it's unclear what information the Receiving Party is obligated to protect, creating a strong in-text contradiction that aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall to obtain Non-Disclosure Agreements containing appropriate restrictions on the persons signing the same prior to disclosing other confidential information (or any concepts, inventions or other information derived there from) to such persons.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party may obtain Non-Disclosure Agreements prior to disclosing other confidential information to such persons.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall' to 'may', the requirement to obtain Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs) is now optional. This contradicts the overall intent of the agreement to safeguard confidential information, especially Section 1 which states an explicit 'Duty to Keep Confidential'. If NDAs are not required, it creates an in-text contradiction about the level of protection expected, as it undermines the 'Duty' outlined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duty to Keep Confidential. Each party receiving other confidential information ("Receiving Party") belonging to or received from the other party (the "Disclosing Party") shall safeguard the Disclosing Party's Other Confidential Information and shall not disclose it (or any concepts, inventions or other information derived there from) to third parties (unless required by any judicial or governmental request, requirement or order) or to use it (or any <PAGE> concepts, inventions or other information derived there from) for any purposes other than evaluation without the express prior written consent of the Disclosing Party set forth in a separate written consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that each party 'shall' obtain NDAs, creating a mandatory obligation. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'may', making the NDA requirement optional. This directly contradicts the 'Duty to Keep Confidential' outlined in the contradicted text, as the duty to safeguard confidential information is undermined if NDAs are not consistently required. This change creates a strong in-text contradiction, as it introduces a significant inconsistency in the protection of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Other Confidential Information (and all concepts, inventions and other information derived there from) shall be deemed, as between the parties hereto, to be the property of the Disclosing party and the Receiving Party will, upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party, return to the Disclosing Party all such other Confidential Information (and any concepts, inventions or other information derived there from) that is maintained in tangible form and destroy all such information maintained in electronic or other intangible media together with all copies or extracts thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Other Confidential Information shall be deemed, as between the parties hereto, to be the property of the Disclosing party and the Receiving Party will, upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party, return to the Disclosing Party all such other Confidential Information that is maintained in tangible form and destroy all such information maintained in electronic or other intangible media together with all copies or extracts thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase '(and all concepts, inventions and other information derived there from)', the obligation to return concepts and inventions related to the Confidential Information is removed. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 1 still states the Receiving Party shall not disclose '(or any concepts, inventions or other information derived there from)' without consent, but the obligation to return these concepts and inventions upon request is now absent. The Receiving Party now retains concepts/inventions, even if the Disclosing Party requests otherwise.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duty to Keep Confidential. Each party receiving other confidential information ("Receiving Party") belonging to or received from the other party (the "Disclosing Party") shall safeguard the Disclosing Party's Other Confidential Information and shall not disclose it (or any concepts, inventions or other information derived there from) to third parties (unless required by any judicial or governmental request, requirement or order) or to use it (or any <PAGE> concepts, inventions or other information derived there from) for any purposes other than evaluation without the express prior written consent of the Disclosing Party set forth in a separate written consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the phrase '(and all concepts, inventions and other information derived there from)' from the obligation to return Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the 'Duty to Keep Confidential' clause, which explicitly states that the Receiving Party shall not disclose '(or any concepts, inventions or other information derived there from)'. The removal of the obligation to return these concepts/inventions creates a conflict, as the Receiving Party is now allowed to retain them even if the Disclosing Party requests their return, while still being bound by the confidentiality of these concepts/inventions.

perturbation.original_text: Each of parties mutually acknowledge that the Disclosing Party makes no representation or Warranty as to the reliability, accuracy or completeness of Confidentiality Information and that any representation or warranty that may be requested or given shall be set forth in a separate writing signed by the party making that representation or warranty.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of parties mutually acknowledge that the Disclosing Party makes no representation or Warranty as to the reliability of Confidentiality Information and that any representation or warranty that may be requested or given shall be set forth in a separate writing signed by the party making that representation or warranty.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'accuracy or completeness' of Confidentiality Information, this creates an in-text contradiction because Section 1 puts a 'Duty to Keep Confidential'. The duty to keep confidential the information is now a vague duty, because it could be deemed now, that it can be inaccurate and incomplete.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duty to Keep Confidential. Each party receiving other confidential information ("Receiving Party") belonging to or received from the other party (the "Disclosing Party") shall safeguard the Disclosing Party's Other Confidential Information and shall not disclose it (or any concepts, inventions or other information derived there from) to third parties (unless required by any judicial or governmental request, requirement or order) or to use it (or any <PAGE> concepts, inventions or other information derived there from) for any purposes other than evaluation without the express prior written consent of the Disclosing Party set forth in a separate written consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Disclosing Party makes no representation or warranty as to the reliability, accuracy, or completeness of Confidentiality Information. The changed text removes 'accuracy or completeness.' The contradicted text establishes a 'Duty to Keep Confidential' which implicitly requires accuracy and completeness. By removing 'accuracy or completeness', the change introduces uncertainty and undermines the 'Duty to Keep Confidential', thus creating a contradiction. The changed text makes the duty to keep confidential now a vague duty, because the Confidential Information could be deemed now, that it can be inaccurate and incomplete.

perturbation.original_text: The parties acknowledge that the breach of threatened breach of this Agreement may result in irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party and that, in addition to its other remedies, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief to restrain any threatened or continued breach of this Agreement. The parties hereby waive any requirement for the posting of a 2 <PAGE> bond or other security in connection with the granting to the Disclosing Party of such injunctive relief.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties acknowledge that the breach of threatened breach of this Agreement may result in irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party and that, in addition to its other remedies, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief to restrain any threatened or continued breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'The parties hereby waive any requirement for the posting of a 2 <PAGE> bond or other security in connection with the granting to the Disclosing Party of such injunctive relief.' there is now uncertainty and potential litigation delays because it is not clear, that a bond is not required. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 6 indicates entitlement to 'injunctive relief,' but now court processes can take much longer.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Injunctive Relief. The parties acknowledge that the breach of threatened breach of this Agreement may result in irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party and that, in addition to its other remedies, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief to restrain any threatened or continued breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly waived the requirement for a bond regarding injunctive relief. The changed text omits this waiver. While the contradicted text also discusses injunctive relief, the removal of the waiver introduces uncertainty and potential delays, creating a conflict with the expectation of straightforward injunctive relief, as highlighted in the Perturbation Explanation. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the contradicted text assumes the process for injunctive relief will be seamless but the changed text now allows for a bond requirement, conflicting with the original intent. This aligns with the definition of Omissions – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the other Confidential Information (and all concepts, inventions or other information derived there from) and this Agreement may not be terminated, modified, amended or waived orally but only through a writing signed by an authorized representative of the party against whom it is sought to be enforced. There are no representations or warrants except as expressly stated herein.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the other Confidential Information and this Agreement may not be terminated, modified, amended or waived orally but only through a writing signed by an authorized representative of the party against whom it is sought to be enforced. There are no representations or warrants except as expressly stated herein.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase '(and all concepts, inventions or other information derived there from)', the 'entire understanding' clause no longer explicitly covers concepts and inventions. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 1, which includes 'concepts, inventions' within the scope of confidential information. The omission suggests that understandings about these items are not fully integrated into the agreement, making their protection ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duty to Keep Confidential. Each party receiving other confidential information ("Receiving Party") belonging to or received from the other party (the "Disclosing Party") shall safeguard the Disclosing Party's Other Confidential Information and shall not disclose it (or any concepts, inventions or other information derived there from) to third parties (unless required by any judicial or governmental request, requirement or order) or to use it (or any <PAGE> concepts, inventions or other information derived there from) for any purposes other than evaluation without the express prior written consent of the Disclosing Party set forth in a separate written consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes '(and all concepts, inventions or other information derived there from)' within the entire understanding clause. The changed text omits this phrase. The contradicted text explicitly includes '(or any concepts, inventions or other information derived there from)' as part of the confidential information that must be safeguarded. By removing the phrase, the changed text creates ambiguity as to whether 'concepts, inventions' are included in the agreement, directly contradicting the contradicted text, which specifically includes them.
